leaves.content: Acme Corp — enterprise SaaS client, onboarded Q3 2025. Primary use case: automated document processing. 12-month contract, renewal discussion scheduled Q2 2026. Key contact: Head of Engineering.
Acme Corp: last contact March 10 2026. Discussed pipeline performance at scale (10k docs/day). Positive — requested benchmark report before renewal meeting.
Brightfield Analytics — mid-market client, data pipeline integration. Technically strong team, prefers async communication. Currently evaluating competitor offering — price sensitive.

COUNTER: [b73cb993ad0e] Confidence in Acme Corp renewal is based on two meetings. Statistically insufficient for a high-confidence renewal forecast. Recommend reducing confidence to 0.65 until benchmark report is delivered and reviewed.

Meridian Health Systems — healthcare vertical, onboarded Jan 2026. Primary use case: clinical trial document classification. Strict HIPAA requirements drove custom data residency config. 24-month contract.
Meridian Health: completed Phase 1 integration — 3,200 documents processed in first 30 days. Zero data-residency violations flagged. Expanding to radiology department Q2 2026.
Pinnacle Financial Group — enterprise fintech client, onboarded Nov 2025. Use case: regulatory filing extraction and cross-referencing. High-touch account requiring weekly sync calls.
TerraForge Industrial — manufacturing sector pilot. Evaluating PCIS for supply chain documentation and vendor compliance tracking. Decision expected by end of Q1 2026.
Brightfield Analytics: renewed for 12 months at 8% discount after competitive evaluation. Decisive factor was Merkle audit trail — competitor lacked tamper-evident logging.
NovaStar Logistics — Series C logistics startup, onboarded Dec 2025. Primary use case: freight compliance document tracking across 14 jurisdictions. 18-month contract with expansion clause.
NovaStar Logistics: Q1 2026 usage review — 8,400 documents processed, 99.2% classification accuracy. Requesting API rate limit increase from 500 to 2,000 req/min.

Titan Financial Services — top-20 US bank, evaluating PCIS for mortgage document processing. Proof of concept approved for 90 days starting Feb 2026. Security review passed with zero critical findings.

Titan Financial: POC milestone 1 complete — processed 12,000 mortgage applications in batch mode. Accuracy: 97.8% on structured fields, 91.3% on free-text clauses. Stakeholder demo scheduled March 15.
Meridian Group Capital — private equity firm, onboarded Oct 2025. Use case: due diligence document ingestion for portfolio company acquisitions. Processes ~200 documents per deal.
Meridian Group: completed 3 deal rooms using PCIS pipeline since onboarding. Average turnaround reduced from 12 days to 4 days. Expanding license to cover London office.

Cascade Biotech — mid-size pharma company, pilot started Jan 2026. Use case: FDA submission document preparation and cross-reference checking. Highly regulated environment with 21 CFR Part 11 requirements.
Vanguard Education Group — edtech holding company managing 6 online universities. Use case: accreditation document management and compliance tracking. 36-month contract signed.
Atlas Manufacturing Co. — Fortune 500 industrial conglomerate. Evaluating PCIS for ISO 9001 quality management documentation across 23 plants. Budget approved; legal review in progress.
Acme Corp: benchmark report delivered March 18. Processing speed: 14,200 docs/day sustained over 72-hour test. Client response positive — renewal meeting moved up to March 28.

COUNTER: [c8b4e6f21a93] Brightfield's renewal at 8% discount sets a precedent. Three other mid-market clients aware of this pricing. Risk of discount cascade in Q3 renewals if not addressed in pricing policy.
Pinnacle Financial: weekly sync cadence reduced to biweekly after process stabilization. Churn risk lowered — NPS score improved from 32 to 67 between Nov 2025 and Feb 2026.
TerraForge Industrial: pilot concluded — decision deferred to Q2 2026 due to internal restructuring. Maintain warm relationship; CTO expressed continued interest pending new budget cycle.

Data residency: all knowledge tree data stored locally, no external API calls made during inference. PCIS architecture satisfies data locality requirements by default — no data leaves the deployment boundary.

Audit trail: every knowledge tree entry contains SHA-256 hash of content, session timestamp, and source reference. Merkle root changes on every update. Full tamper-evident history available by replaying root hash sequence.

COUNTER: [6c1d120c878a] 'No external API calls' is an architectural property of the current build, not a guarantee. Any dependency update or plugin integration could introduce external calls silently. This leaf requires a periodic re-verification trigger — recommend automated test that intercepts all outbound network calls on each release.
SOC 2 Type II audit scheduled for Q2 2026. Pre-audit checklist completed — 14 of 16 controls verified. Remaining gaps: formal incident response runbook and third-party penetration test.
GDPR Article 17 compliance: right-to-erasure implemented via leaf pruning with Merkle recomputation. Deletion is cryptographically verifiable — pruned leaf hash absent from subsequent root.

ISO 27001 gap analysis complete. Primary finding: need formalized access control matrix for knowledge tree write operations. Read access is unrestricted by design; write access requires session authentication.

Data retention policy: knowledge tree leaves retained indefinitely unless explicitly pruned. Counter-leaves and low-confidence entries flagged for quarterly review. No automatic expiration to preserve institutional memory.

Healthcare client deployments must pass HIPAA BAA requirements before go-live. Checklist includes: encryption at rest (AES-256), audit logging enabled, no PHI in leaf content without de-identification.
FedRAMP Moderate authorization pathway under evaluation for government sector clients. Estimated 8-month timeline. Blocker: need dedicated GovCloud deployment infrastructure.

PCI DSS v4.0 compliance assessment initiated for payment-adjacent clients. PCIS does not store cardholder data directly, but document ingestion pipelines may contain PAN references requiring tokenization.

Penetration test completed by CyberShield Labs, Feb 2026. No critical or high-severity findings. Two medium findings: verbose error messages in API responses and missing rate limiting on /api/search endpoint.

COUNTER: [a3e7f12bc945] SOC 2 pre-audit checklist marks 14/16 controls as verified, but two missing controls — incident response and pen test — are among the most frequently cited in audit failures. Confidence should not exceed 0.75 until both gaps are closed.
FedRAMP Moderate authorization exploration started. Initial gap analysis shows 47 of 325 controls require implementation. Estimated 6-month effort with dedicated compliance engineer.
Data processing agreements (DPAs) standardized across all EU clients. Template covers GDPR Articles 28, 32, and 33. Legal approved v2.1 of DPA template on March 5, 2026.
Vulnerability disclosure policy published at /security.txt. Coordinated disclosure window set to 90 days. Bug bounty program under evaluation — budget request submitted for Q3 2026.

Annual security awareness training completed for all engineering staff. 100% completion rate. Topics covered: supply chain attacks, social engineering, secure coding practices, and incident escalation procedures.
Client data classification framework adopted: Tier 1 (PII/PHI), Tier 2 (business confidential), Tier 3 (public). Knowledge tree leaves auto-tagged based on source branch and content analysis.
Cross-border data transfer mechanisms reviewed. Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) implemented for EU-US transfers. UK Addendum applied for post-Brexit compliance.
Backup and disaster recovery tested. RTO: 4 hours, RPO: 1 hour. Knowledge tree restored from encrypted S3 snapshot in 47 minutes during tabletop exercise.

Demo performance: VP-level presentations respond better to live demo than slides. Boot integrity check in real time (Merkle root computed on screen) is the highest-impact moment — do not skip it or pre-load results.

Knowledge tree commits: significant results must be committed to the tree at session end, not deferred. Daily notes get compressed; the tree is permanent. If a benchmark, decision, or proof matters — it goes in the tree immediately.

Model-agnostic framing resonates strongly with enterprise buyers. The question they ask is always 'what happens when the model changes?' — having a concrete answer (swap the model, memory layer is unchanged) closes that objection immediately.

COUNTER: [248c720c4e3a] 'Live demo always beats slides' is a pattern from a small sample of presentations. Some audiences (highly technical, time-constrained) prefer a structured slide deck they can reference later. Recommendation: lead with demo, have slides available for follow-up. Do not assume demo-first is universally optimal.

COUNTER: [c22d36fc0bf6] The 'commit immediately' rule is correct but incomplete. Committing every result without curation leads to tree bloat. The discipline is: commit significant results immediately AND prune low-confidence or superseded leaves at the end of each session. Both halves are required.

Onboarding new clients: always run the adversarial gardener on their first knowledge branch within 48 hours. Early counter-leaves set the expectation that the system challenges its own beliefs — this builds trust faster than perfection.

Production incident 2026-02-08: tree file corruption from concurrent writes during load test. Root cause was missing file lock. Fixed by adding fcntl.LOCK_EX to save_tree. Lesson: always assume multi-process access even in 'single-user' deployments.

Embedding model cold-start adds 4-6 seconds to first semantic search query. Pre-pulling the model in setup.sh reduced perceived latency to zero for demos. Always warm the model cache before any live presentation.
Client-facing tree exports should omit counter-leaves by default. Counter-leaves are internal epistemic hygiene — exposing them to clients without context creates confusion about system confidence.

Lesson: batch ingestion of 50k+ documents causes memory pressure on 8GB instances. Discovered during Titan Financial POC. Mitigation: streaming mode with 500-doc chunks and explicit gc.collect() between batches.
Lesson: client onboarding time directly correlates with data format diversity. Clients with >5 input formats average 3.2 weeks to full integration vs 1.1 weeks for single-format clients.
Lesson: adversarial validation catches real issues. March 2026 gardener run identified 3 leaves with confidence scores unsupported by evidence. Two were corrected; one was pruned entirely.

COUNTER: [prev-lesson-general] Relying solely on confidence scores for leaf quality is insufficient. A leaf can be high-confidence but outdated, or low-confidence but accurately reflecting genuine uncertainty.
Lesson: demo environment divergence from production caused a client-facing bug in Jan 2026. Demo tree had stale schema; production had migrated. Now enforcing schema version checks on all tree loads.
Lesson: semantic search quality degrades when leaf content exceeds 500 characters. Embedding models compress long text poorly. Best practice: split complex knowledge into multiple focused leaves.

Lesson: weekly syncs with high-touch clients (Pinnacle Financial) were unsustainable at scale. Shifted to async Loom updates + biweekly live calls. Client satisfaction unchanged; team capacity recovered 6 hrs/week.

Lesson: Merkle root verification on every API request adds ~2ms latency. Acceptable for integrity-critical endpoints but unnecessary for read-only queries. Moved to verify-on-write + periodic background checks.

Lesson: belief decay with 180-day half-life was too aggressive for compliance leaves. Regulatory facts don't decay like market intelligence. Implemented per-branch decay profiles: compliance=360d, clients=120d, risk=90d.

Lesson: PDF extraction via pdftotext fails silently on scanned documents without OCR layer. Two client uploads returned empty strings. Added content-length validation post-extraction with fallback to Tesseract OCR.

Lesson: synapse graph becomes unwieldy beyond 200 connections. Query time for belief traversal increased 4x between 150 and 250 synapses. Implemented connection pruning for low-weight edges during quarterly maintenance.

Lesson: never merge client-specific customizations into the main product branch. Meridian Health's HIPAA configuration leaked into the default setup, temporarily enabling unnecessary audit logging for all clients.

Core API v2.3: Merkle integrity verification p99 236ms (300-agent async simulation), memory footprint 346 MB. Benchmarked on isolated test cluster, March 2026. Not end-to-end LLM response latency. Confidence valid for current architecture — recalibrate after any infrastructure change.
Knowledge tree query: semantic search via nomic-embed-text embeddings returns results in <100ms for trees up to 10,000 leaves. Keyword fallback available when Ollama is unavailable.
Merkle integrity verification adds ~12ms overhead on boot. Acceptable for most deployments. For latency-critical paths, integrity check can be deferred to background thread after initial boot.

COUNTER: [f8b9e375c48e] Benchmark confidence 0.92 is high for a single test run on isolated infrastructure. Production environments introduce variability (network jitter, contention, GC pauses). Recommend tagging this as 'lab-condition benchmark' and scheduling a production load test before quoting to enterprise clients.

VR visualization module: Three.js-based 3D rendering of knowledge tree. Branch nodes rendered as orbital clusters, leaves as interactive spheres color-coded by confidence. Supports up to 500 leaves at 60fps on M-series hardware.
Tree diff engine: cross-instance comparison completes in O(n log n) for balanced trees. Tested with two 5,000-leaf instances — full diff computed in 180ms including network serialization overhead.

Plugin architecture: knowledge tree accepts custom branch types via JSON schema validation. Third-party integrations (Jira, Linear, Confluence) import external artifacts as read-only leaves with provenance tracking.

Embedding index rebuild: full reindex of 10,000 leaves completes in 47 seconds on 8-core machine. Incremental indexing adds <200ms per new leaf. Index file size grows linearly at approximately 1.2 KB per leaf.

Roadmap Q3 2026: federated tree sync protocol for multi-site deployments. Design goal is eventual consistency with conflict resolution via timestamp + confidence weighting. Prototype targets two-node setup.
Product: Knowledge Tree v2.1 shipped Jan 2026. Key features: streaming ingestion, per-branch decay profiles, and improved PDF extraction with OCR fallback. 23 clients upgraded within first two weeks.
Product: Semantic Search module powered by nomic-embed-text via Ollama. Supports top-k retrieval with branch filtering. Falls back to keyword matching when embedding service unavailable.
Product: Belief Traversal engine — graph-based confidence assessment using synapse connections. Classifies leaves as CONFIDENT, UNCERTAIN, CONTESTED, or SUPERSEDED based on network position.
Product roadmap Q2 2026: FedRAMP Moderate certification, multi-tenant knowledge trees, real-time collaboration on leaf editing, and a Slack integration for ingestion notifications.
Product: API rate limiting implemented at 1,000 req/min per client. Burst allowance: 50 requests in 1 second. Clients exceeding limits receive 429 with Retry-After header.

COUNTER: [product-roadmap] Multi-tenant knowledge trees require fundamental schema changes. Current Merkle structure assumes single-root integrity. Sharing branches across tenants breaks the tamper-evidence guarantee without a per-tenant root.

Product: Adversarial Gardener v1.0 — automated counter-leaf generation using LLM-driven challenge prompts. Runs weekly against all high-confidence leaves. March 2026 run challenged 12 leaves, lowered confidence on 4.
Product: Document Ingestion pipeline supports PDF, TXT, and DOCX. Extracts 10-20 factual claims per document via LLM. Each claim committed as a Merkle-anchored leaf with source provenance.
Product: Docker self-contained demo image published to internal registry. Includes Ollama, pre-loaded models, demo tree with 45 leaves, and all API endpoints. Boot time: ~30 seconds on M2 Mac.
Product: Belief History module tracks all confidence changes per leaf over time. Supports version diffing, timeline visualization, and rollback to previous confidence states.
Product: Knowledge Synapses graph connects related leaves with typed relations: SUPPORTS, CONTRADICTS, REFINES, DERIVES_FROM. Used by Belief Traversal for network-aware confidence scoring.

Acme Corp renewal risk: benchmark report not yet delivered. If report shows degraded performance vs. original demo, renewal probability drops significantly. Mitigation: schedule internal test run this week.

Brightfield competitive risk: actively evaluating a lower-cost alternative. Price gap is approximately 20%. Value proposition must be demonstrated concretely — raw capability comparison alone will not win this account.

COUNTER: [54cc0d2517a7] Confidence 0.72 on competitive risk assessment is based on a single conversation. Customer stated they were 'evaluating' — this could be a negotiation signal rather than a genuine competitive threat. Recommend direct follow-up to clarify timeline and decision criteria before adjusting strategy.

Single-point-of-failure risk: knowledge tree currently stored as single JSON file on local disk. Disk failure or accidental deletion results in total data loss. Mitigation: implement automated nightly backup to encrypted S3 bucket.
Model dependency risk: nomic-embed-text model hosted via Ollama could be deprecated or have breaking changes. Mitigation: pin model version in setup.sh and maintain fallback to keyword search.

Scalability ceiling: current JSON-file-based storage will degrade beyond ~50,000 leaves. Estimated threshold based on file I/O benchmarks. Migration path to SQLite or embedded DB should be designed before any client exceeds 20,000 leaves.

Regulatory risk: upcoming EU AI Act (effective Aug 2026) may classify knowledge tree systems as 'limited risk AI'. Requires transparency obligations — users must be informed when interacting with AI-curated knowledge. Legal review initiated.

Meridian Health onboarding risk: HIPAA compliance validation not yet independently audited. Self-assessment passed but client's legal team may require third-party attestation before Phase 2 expansion.

Open question: adversarial gardener's counter-leaf generation could be gamed by crafting leaf content that triggers false positives. No exploit demonstrated yet, but the attack surface exists. Recommend fuzzing the gardener with adversarial inputs.

Risk: single-point-of-failure on Ollama embedding service. If Ollama is down, semantic search degrades to keyword fallback. No automatic failover to alternative embedding providers currently configured.
Risk: key-person dependency on lead engineer for Merkle tree internals. Only one team member can debug hash verification failures end-to-end. Knowledge transfer sessions scheduled for Q2 2026.
Risk: client concentration — top 3 clients (Acme, Meridian Health, Vanguard) represent 58% of ARR. Loss of any single account would require immediate cost restructuring.

Risk: regulatory landscape shifting — EU AI Act enforcement begins Aug 2026. PCIS may be classified as high-risk if clients use it for employment or creditworthiness decisions. Legal analysis in progress.

COUNTER: [risk-ollama] Keyword fallback is not a true mitigation — it returns qualitatively different results. Clients relying on semantic search for compliance workflows would experience silent degradation, not a graceful fallback.
Risk: demo tree divergence from production schema. Demo uses flat JSON; production considering migration to SQLite for performance. Schema mismatch could cause integration test blind spots.
Risk: LLM model dependency — claim extraction relies on qwen3:14b via Ollama. Model updates could alter extraction quality without warning. No regression test suite for extraction accuracy.
Risk: open-source supply chain — 14 Python dependencies in requirements.txt. Last dependency audit: Feb 2026. One transitive dependency (PyPDF2) is unmaintained; evaluating pypdf as replacement.
Risk: competitive pressure from DocuMind AI, which raised $40M Series B in Jan 2026. Their product targets the same enterprise document processing market but lacks Merkle integrity features.
